design_basis: Derived from observed artifacts: 1,112 normalized shade records across 10 lines / 5 brands (Batches 1-2), 10 line technical records, 356 tone reference rows, 353 tone mappings, 152 cataloged sources. Key observed shapes that drive the design: (a) three distinct shade-code grammars (Redken/Matrix letter codes, Wella slash digits, SKP hyphen digits, L'Oréal dot digits); (b) tone codes are line-scoped, not brand-scoped; (c) the same shade_code can repeat within a line across sub-ranges (Matrix Extra Coverage vs DreamAge 504N); (d) per-shade technical values are sparse while line-level defaults are dense; (e) every fact needs source provenance with confidence and evidence enums.

fields.rationale: Region is part of the canonical key because identical lines differ by market (e.g., Dia Richesse US phase-out vs other markets; Pulp Riot UK discontinuation).
Needed because shade_code alone is not unique within a line (Matrix 504N exists in both Extra Coverage and DreamAge).
Immutable, append-only copy of manufacturer wording. Never updated in place; superseded rows are linked via record_version.
The normalized, query-facing record. Inheritance flags preserve the raw-vs-derived distinction observed in Stage 8 assumptions.
Ordered many-to-many; digit systems are positional (Wella /43 = primary Red, secondary Gold).
Tone codes are line-scoped: SEQ 'NB' ≠ CGL 'RB' meaning; cross-line reuse must stay explicit, matching Stage 6 output.
Auditable many-to-many from Stage 7; multiple normalized tones per manufacturer code are the norm (e.g., SKP -1 Cendré → Ash+Blue+Violet).

Line-level rules observed in Stage 5 are heterogeneous (tables of developer strengths, white-blending tables, booster dosing); JSON values with typed rule_type keep them queryable without forcing premature structure.
content_hash enables change detection on re-crawl.
Generic provenance join so every meaningful record can carry 1..n sources, matching the multi-source reality of Stage 4 (leaflet + factsheet + distributor).

foreign_keys: shade_raw.line_region_id -> product_line_region; shade_raw.source_id -> source_document; shade_raw.ingestion_run_id -> ingestion_run
shade.shade_raw_id -> shade_raw; shade.line_region_id -> product_line_region; shade.sub_range_id -> sub_range
relationships: tone_code_reference N:M normalized_tone (via tone_normalization, with rationale + confidence)
product_line_region 1:N line_technical_rule; shade inherits rule values only via explicit inherited_from_line flags
every fact table N:M source_document via record_source
shade_raw 1:N shade versions over time via record_version
schema_proposal.raw_vs_normalized_strategy: Two-layer model. shade_raw is append-only and preserves exact manufacturer wording (codes, names, claims) per source; shade is the normalized layer rebuilt deterministically from shade_raw + line_technical_rule + tone_normalization. Normalized values never overwrite raw values; derived/inherited fields carry inherited_from_line flags and the Stage-8 assumption text. Re-normalization is a pure function of the raw layer, so mapping-table fixes can be replayed without re-crawling.
schema_proposal.provenance_model: Every shade_raw, tone_code_reference, and line_technical_rule row references a source_document directly; record_source adds corroborating/conflicting sources. source_document keeps tier (1=manufacturer, 2=distributor, 3=other), access_status, retrieval timestamp, and content hash. Conflicts are stored as additional record_source rows with role=conflicting plus an issue_log entry — never silently merged, mirroring the Stage 4 source_conflicts artifact.
schema_proposal.versioning_model: Append-only raw layer + record_version table providing valid_from/valid_to ranges and superseded_by chains, keyed to ingestion_run. Supports: shade discontinuation (is_active=false with version history retained), chart revisions (e.g., Majirel 07/23 vs 09/25 charts both observed in Batch 2), and region divergence (same line, different shade sets per market).
schema_proposal.brand_adapter_architecture: Per-line ingestion adapters declared by code_grammar: (1) letter_suffix parser (Redken/Matrix; level prefix + letter tone codes; needs line-scoped letter table), (2) slash_digits parser (Wella x/yz), (3) hyphen_digits parser (SKP x-yz incl. ',5' half levels and 3-digit Absolutes), (4) dot_digits parser (L'Oréal x.yz). Each adapter outputs the same shade_raw shape plus parser_confidence; tone decoding is data-driven from tone_code_reference rather than hardcoded, so new lines only need a legend ingest, not new code. Adapters must never share tone tables across lines without an explicit inferred-mapping row (lesson from SEQ vs CGL letter reuse).
schema_proposal.ingestion_update_workflow: 1) Stage-2 style source discovery per line -> source_document rows; 2) fetch + hash; skip unchanged hashes; 3) adapter parse -> shade_raw append (new ingestion_run); 4) diff against current shade set -> new/changed/missing shades; missing shades flagged candidate-discontinued, never auto-deleted; 5) tone legend diff -> tone_code_reference updates with confidence; 6) deterministic re-normalization -> shade layer; 7) QA suite (duplicate codes per sub_range, level/code mismatch, unmapped tone codes, count-vs-claim) -> issue_log; 8) human review queue for conflicting/unresolved rows.
architectural_risks: Sub-range modeling is load-bearing: without it, UNIQUE(line, shade_code) fails on real data (Matrix Extra Coverage vs DreamAge collisions).
Tone normalization is interpretive for ~15% of codes (inferred/flagged rows); downstream formula conversion must read mapping_confidence and ambiguity_flag, not just normalized tones.
Manufacturer chart recency varies (2020-2025 PDFs observed); freshness must be tracked per source, not per brand, or stale shades will look authoritative.
Letter-code grammars are not regular across brands (ABn, NCh, VRo mixed-case multi-letter codes); the parser needs per-line token tables, not a global regex.
JSON rule_value in line_technical_rule trades queryability for fidelity; if formula generation later needs structured developer math, promote mixing/developer rules to typed columns.
Region as part of the canonical key multiplies rows for global lines; without it, US-vs-EU shade set differences (already observed for Wella and Pulp Riot) become silent conflicts.

issue_log.details: Proposal is design-only; no DDL has been applied to a database in this pass. The existing repo's primary_db/.csv flat files are a separate legacy structure not yet migrated.
